text: The laparoscopic view shows the inguinal hernia defect with bilateral H, and the iliopubic ligament is visible on the right side of the screen.

The surgeon sews the iliopubic ligament to the abdominal wall muscle. The peritoneum, the inner layer of the abdominal wall, is opened, revealing the preperitoneal space. The hernia sac and its contents, including fat, have been removed, and the hernia defect is clearly visible.

The video shows the inguinal hernia in a female patient, with the round ligament visible on the left side, passing through the hernia defect. The surgeon completes the repair of the hernia defect using absorbable sutures.
The surgeon closes the peritoneum with absorbable suture at the end of the hernia repair procedure.
